pages.raw_text:
AS was done in nearly every large
city throughout the world yester-
day, May day, known as interna.
tional labor day, the socialists OF
Spokane assembled to discuss their
propaganda. At Mantle park fully
1900 attended the meeting and lis
tened to several addresses, the prin-
cipal speakers being d. G. Coates.
who presided, Rev. Henry Victor
Morgan of Portland and Attorney
Frank II. Moore.


Park officials have granted the
local socialists the use of Mantle
park throughout the summer for
weekly meetings, and they were
praised by the speakers for their
action. The meeting was a quiet
one. Papers were circulated for
signers to a petition to have the
city charter revised and some pam
phlets on the cost of living were
distributed.


The topic of Rev. Morgan was
